page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server within the Bare Metal NVMe family, designed to provide dedicated hardware resources without virtualization overhead. The system is built on the x86_64 architecture and features an AMD EPYC 9255 processor. This CPU configuration delivers 24 physical cores and 48 threads operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz, ensuring consistent compute performance for multi-threaded applications.

The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided by a 960 GB NVMe SSD, which facilitates fast read and write operations. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration, meaning all parallel processing and computational tasks must rely entirely on the host CPU.

Given its high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated hardware allocation, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC processing, substantial RAM, and local NVMe storage makes this configuration suitable for database management, enterprise application hosting, and general-purpose workloads requiring predictable physical resource availability.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a dedicated bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9255 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, featuring 24 physical cores and 48 threads running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage consists of a 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no GPUs integrated into this hardware profile. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring dedicated physical resources and substantial memory capacity without the need for hardware-accelerated graphics. It is suitable for database management systems, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and general-purpose server operations that benefit from the elimination of virtualization overhead.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC 9255 processor, 384 GB of RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database hosting.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring AMD EPYC processing, high-capacity system memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding workloads.

384.0 GB of dedicated system memory for memory-intensive applications
